data.title: J&J upgraded at Wells Fargo on valuation, pharma potential
data.description:
• Wells Fargo upgraded Johnson & Johnson  JNJ) to over-weight from equal weight saying that the healthcare giant is attractively priced with pharmaceutical tariff risks and drug pricing concerns in the rear-view mirror.
  • The bank upped its price target to $212 from $170 (~14% upside based on Oct. 2 close).
  • Analyst Larry Biegelsen said that J&J's current share price provides an attractive entry point given the potential upside to its pharma business.
  • In that segment, he expects better growth in 2026 than 2025, and in 2027, to exceed 2026's growth.
  • Biegelsen added that in light of Pfizer's (PFE) announcement on Sept. 30 of a deal with the Trump administration to supply its drugs at a discount to US consumers, he expects other pharmas to follow suit.

data.title: Mortgage rates rises to 6.34% in last week
data.description:
Mortgage rates increased again this week but remains below its 52-week average of 6.71%, according to the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

The 30-year FRM averaged 6.34% as of October 2, 2025, up from last week when it averaged 6.30%. A year ago at this time, the 30-year FRM averaged 6.12%.

The 15-year FRM averaged 5.55%, up from last week when it averaged 5.49%. A year ago at this time, the 15-year FRM averaged 5.25%.

“The last few months have brought lower rates and as indicated by the recently reported increase in pending home sales, homebuyers are feeling more confident to get into the market," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

data.title: Tesla begins Cybertruck sales in Qatar
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is set to launch Cybertruck sales in Qatar, marking its latest step in a Middle East expansion that follows the April debut of its first showroom and service center in Saudi Arabia.

The company’s expansion into the oil-rich region – which began in 2017 in the United Arab Emirates -- hopes to counter increasing competition in Europe from cheaper Chinese models, and in the U.S. where EV sales are expected to be hampered by the end of government tax credits.

On Thursday, the company said it delivered nearly 500K vehicles in the third quarter, beating quarterly records and exceeding Wall Street’s expectations.

Although the surge in sales was most likely fueled by the end of the $7,500 tax credit, the news drove shares to their highest level this year before surrendering gains throughout the rest of the day.

Into the open, shares are back in the plus column, gaining 1.4% in premarket trading.

data.title: AerCap leased, purchased and sold 146 assets in Q3
data.description:
• AerCap Holdings N.V. (NYSE:AER) has signed 66 lease agreements, including 14 widebody aircraft, 25 narrowbody aircraft, 15 engines and 12 helicopters in the third quarter of 2025.
  • The company completed 35 purchases for 17 aircraft (including 8 Airbus A320neo Family aircraft and 9 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft for AerCap's owned aircraft portfolio), 11 engines for AerCap's owned engine portfolio and 7 helicopters.
  • The company also completed 45 sale transactions for 40 aircraft (including 2 Airbus A320 Family aircraft, 10 Airbus A320neo Family aircraft, 1 Airbus A330, 5 Boeing 737NGs, 7 Boeing 777-300ERs and 5 Embraer E190/E195s from AerCap's owned aircraft portfolio and 10 aircraft from AerCap's managed aircraft portfolio), 4 engines (including 1 engine from AerCap's owned engine portfolio and 3 engines from AerCap's managed engine portfolio) and 1 helicopter.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GameSquare begins $5M stock buyback program with initial repurchase
data.description:
• GameSquare Holdings (NASDAQ:GAME) repurchased 833,124 shares for $599,148.
  • The average repurchase price was ~$0.7192 per share.
  • $4.4M remains under the company's $5M buyback authorization.
  • Repurchases are funded through GameSquare’s treasury strategy.
  • Source: Press release
